content: We can frame LLM self-reflection mechanisms as a computational implementation of the linguistic concept of reflexion as 'reflecting on one's own reflection', where the common educational practice of having students reflect on their own written arguments can be borrowed to improve LLM self-reflection. Specifically, asking LLMs to explicitly reflect on their own reflected outputs (just as high school writing workshops ask students to reflect on their drafted reflections during writing practice) creates a recursive reinforcement loop that reduces reasoning inconsistencies better than single-perspective self-reflection.
